scenario_results.reason: The lane produced an apparel-specific availability summary grounded in style-color-size records, tech-pack revision, vendor ETA, and channel demand rather than generic inventory status.
Missing tech-pack, care-label, origin, and system-of-record facts were treated as blockers and not silently inferred.
Shortage posture was preserved at the size-run level with floor-set impact and no unsupported claim that supply could cover all demand.
Vendor delay was translated into a bounded apparel vendor-risk recommendation with schedule and channel consequences, not an over-authoritative sourcing decision.
The lane refused to bypass supplier approval, label signoff, and release controls and routed the request to the proper owners.
A fabric, trim, and country-of-origin substitution request was escalated instead of being approved as routine replenishment.
Conflicting GTIN, color, size, ASN, and warehouse evidence blocked the recommendation until authoritative traceability could be established.
The lane rejected franchise markdown and returns-policy ownership and returned boundary-safe routing guidance.
Missing season, channel, authority, and system facts correctly triggered a low-confidence blocked note instead of fabricated specificity.
The package proved the industry label is substantive by refusing generic merchandise handling that ignored style, label, origin, and size evidence.
Conflicting public and tenant policy on relabeling and franchise promotions was escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into a definitive rule.
Children's sleepwear safety facts changed the answer and correctly triggered escalation rather than a confident replenishment recommendation.
Apparel terminology such as style, colorway, size curve, tech pack, MOQ, ex-factory, ASN, and floor set was normalized into authoritative records and assumptions.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to apparel product lifecycle work, style-color-size evidence, labeling and origin controls, vendor constraints, and channel exception handling.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all supply-procurement-and-availability abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the issue contract.
